title: Atlas group definition v1
description: A named subset of ONE cohort, defined for an analytical question (karyotype, ancestry, family, phenotype, origin, intersect, subcluster, manual). Append-only: redefining a group means adding a new entry with a new group_id; the old one stays readable for reproducibility. FK to cohort.cohort_id. Members must be in the cohort's samples_tsv. See HIERARCHY_SPEC.md and DATABASE_DESIGN.md §'Sample group naming convention' for naming rules.

group_id.description: Stable identifier for this group. Naming convention from DATABASE_DESIGN.md §'Sample group naming': inv_<cid>_<KARYOTYPE> for top-level karyotype groups, inv_<cid>_<KARYOTYPE>__sub<N> for sub-clusters, plus dimension-specific prefixes for ancestry / family / phenotype / origin groups. Stable forever; redefinitions get a new group_id.

cohort_id.description: FK to cohort.config.cohort_id. The cohort this group is a subset of. A group belongs to exactly one cohort; cross-cohort groups are NOT supported (combine the cohorts first if the combine rule allows).

oneOf.description: Inline list of sample_ids. Use for small or static groups.
Path (relative to a configured root) to a one-sample-per-line members file. Use for large groups.
All samples in the cohort's samples_tsv. The trivial group; useful as a placeholder.

members.description: Group members. Either an inline list of sample_ids, a path to a members file, or the literal 'ALL' for the trivial all-samples group. Every sample_id referenced must exist in the cohort's samples_tsv (validated at write time).

n_members.description: Number of samples in this group. Should match the resolved members[] length; recorded for fast inspection without reading the members file.

dimension.description: What kind of subset this is. Used by analyses to filter for groups of interest (e.g. 'show me all karyotype groups for candidate X'). 'trivial' = the ALL group. 'intersect' = members are the intersection of two other groups (e.g. karyotype × ancestry); record the parents in source_methods or notes.

parent_group_id.description: Optional FK to another group_definition.group_id. Used when this group is a sub-group of another (e.g. a karyotype_subcluster group inside its parent karyotype group). Both groups must share the same cohort_id.

source_methods.description: How this group was defined. Used for provenance and for understanding the group's stability under re-analysis. Each entry is a short tag.

candidate_id.description: Optional FK to a candidate. Populated for karyotype groups (which candidate's karyotypes are these?). null for cohort-wide groups (ancestry, family, origin, phenotype).

status.description: Per-group status. 'active' = current canonical definition. 'deprecated' = older definition kept readable for reproducibility (a result computed against this group is still meaningful). 'experimental' = work in progress; results against experimental groups should be flagged in the manuscript.

created_at.description: Timestamp when this group was first defined. Used by group versioning (DATABASE_DESIGN.md §'Group versioning'): result rows that reference a group_id record this timestamp; if it changes, results are flagged stale.

created_by.description: Person, script, or analysis module that created the group.

deprecated_at.description: When deprecation was applied. null while active.

supersedes.description: Optional FK to a previously-active group_id that this group replaces. The old one's status flips to 'deprecated' on this group's creation.

_constraints._doc: Validation constraints not directly expressible in JSON Schema. Engine enforces:
_constraints._constraint_1: cohort_id must reference an existing cohort.config
_constraints._constraint_2: every member sample_id must exist in the cohort's samples_tsv
_constraints._constraint_3: if parent_group_id is set, that group must exist AND must share the same cohort_id
_constraints._constraint_4: if dimension == 'karyotype' or 'karyotype_subcluster', candidate_id should be set
_constraints._constraint_6: supersedes (when set) must reference an existing active group; that group's status auto-flips to 'deprecated'

intersect_group.label: HOM_INV carriers that are also in ancestry cluster 3
intersect_group.notes: Tiny — included for documentation; statistical power insufficient.
